briefdescription.para: Device initialization is deferred.
Type used to represent a "handle" for a device.

detaileddescription.para: Every device has an associated handle. You can get a pointer to a device from its handle and vice versa, but the handle uses less space than a pointer. The device.h API mainly uses handles to store lists of multiple devices in a compact way.
The extreme values and zero have special significance. Negative values identify functionality that does not correspond to a Zephyr device, such as the system clock or a SYS_INIT() function.

briefdescription.para: Device flags.

memberdef.definition: typedef int(* device_visitor_callback_t) (const struct device *dev, void *context)
memberdef.name: device_visitor_callback_t

briefdescription.para: Prototype for functions used when iterating over a set of devices.

detaileddescription.para: Such a function may be used in API that identifies a set of devices and provides a visitor API supporting caller-specific interaction with each device in the set.
The visit is said to succeed if the visitor returns a non-negative value.

parameterdescription.para: a device in the set being iterated
state used to support the visitor function

simplesect.para: A non-negative number to allow walking to continue, and a negative error code to case the iteration to stop.

briefdescription.para: Get the handle for a given device.

parameterdescription.para: the device for which a handle is desired.

simplesect.para: the handle for the device, or DEVICE_HANDLE_NULL if the device does not have an associated handle.

briefdescription.para: Get the device corresponding to a handle.

parameterdescription.para: the device handle

simplesect.para: the device that has that handle, or a null pointer if dev_handle does not identify a device.

briefdescription.para: Get the device handles for devicetree dependencies of this device.

detaileddescription.para: This function returns a pointer to an array of device handles. The length of the array is stored in the count parameter.
The array contains a handle for each device that dev requires directly, as determined from the devicetree. This does not include transitive dependencies; you must recursively determine those.

parameterdescription.para: the device for which dependencies are desired.

pointer to where this function should store the length of the returned array. No value is stored if the call returns a null pointer. The value may be set to zero if the device has no devicetree dependencies.

simplesect.para: a pointer to a sequence of count device handles, or a null pointer if dev does not have any dependency data.

briefdescription.para: Get the device handles for injected dependencies of this device.

detaileddescription.para: This function returns a pointer to an array of device handles. The length of the array is stored in the count parameter.

The array contains a handle for each device that dev manually injected as a dependency, via providing extra arguments to Z_DEVICE_DEFINE. This does not include transitive dependencies; you must recursively determine those.

parameterdescription.para: the device for which injected dependencies are desired.

pointer to where this function should store the length of the returned array. No value is stored if the call returns a null pointer. The value may be set to zero if the device has no devicetree dependencies.

simplesect.para: a pointer to a sequence of *count device handles, or a null pointer if dev does not have any dependency data.

briefdescription.para: Get the set of handles that this device supports.

detaileddescription.para: This function returns a pointer to an array of device handles. The length of the array is stored in the count parameter.

The array contains a handle for each device that dev "supports" that is, devices that require dev directly as determined from the devicetree. This does not include transitive dependencies; you must recursively determine those.

parameterdescription.para: the device for which supports are desired.

pointer to where this function should store the length of the returned array. No value is stored if the call returns a null pointer. The value may be set to zero if nothing in the devicetree depends on dev.

simplesect.para: a pointer to a sequence of *count device handles, or a null pointer if dev does not have any dependency data.

briefdescription.para: Visit every device that dev directly requires.

detaileddescription.para: Zephyr maintains information about which devices are directly required by another device; for example an I2C-based sensor driver will require an I2C controller for communication. Required devices can derive from statically-defined devicetree relationships or dependencies registered at runtime.

This API supports operating on the set of required devices. Example uses include making sure required devices are ready before the requiring device is used, and releasing them when the requiring device is no longer needed.
There is no guarantee on the order in which required devices are visited.
If the visitor_cb function returns a negative value iteration is halted, and the returned value from the visitor is returned from this function.

simplesect.para: This API is not available to unprivileged threads.

parameterdescription.para: a device of interest. The devices that this device depends on will be used as the set of devices to visit. This parameter must not be null.
the function that should be invoked on each device in the dependency set. This parameter must not be null.
state that is passed through to the visitor function. This parameter may be null if visitor_cb tolerates a null context.

simplesect.para: The number of devices that were visited if all visits succeed, or the negative value returned from the first visit that did not succeed.

briefdescription.para: Visit every device that dev directly supports.

detaileddescription.para: Zephyr maintains information about which devices are directly supported by another device; for example an I2C controller will support an I2C-based sensor driver. Supported devices can derive from statically-defined devicetree relationships.
This API supports operating on the set of supported devices. Example uses include iterating over the devices connected to a regulator when it is powered on.
There is no guarantee on the order in which required devices are visited.
If the visitor_cb function returns a negative value iteration is halted, and the returned value from the visitor is returned from this function.

simplesect.para: This API is not available to unprivileged threads.

parameterdescription.para: a device of interest. The devices that this device supports will be used as the set of devices to visit. This parameter must not be null.
the function that should be invoked on each device in the support set. This parameter must not be null.
state that is passed through to the visitor function. This parameter may be null if visitor_cb tolerates a null context.

simplesect.para: The number of devices that were visited if all visits succeed, or the negative value returned from the first visit that did not succeed.

briefdescription.para: Get a device reference from its device::name field.

detaileddescription.para: This function iterates through the devices on the system. If a device with the given name field is found, and that device initialized successfully at boot time, this function returns a pointer to the device.
If no device has the given name, this function returns NULL.

This function also returns NULL when a device is found, but it failed to initialize successfully at boot time. (To troubleshoot this case, set a breakpoint on your device driver's initialization function.)

parameterdescription.para: device name to search for. A null pointer, or a pointer to an empty string, will cause NULL to be returned.

simplesect.para: pointer to device structure with the given name; NULL if the device is not found or if the device with that name's initialization function failed.

briefdescription.para: Verify that a device is ready for use.

detaileddescription.para: Indicates whether the provided device pointer is for a device known to be in a state where it can be used with its standard API.

This can be used with device pointers captured from DEVICE_DT_GET(), which does not include the readiness checks of device_get_binding(). At minimum this means that the device has been successfully initialized.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the device in question.
If the device is ready for use.
If the device is not ready for use or if a NULL device pointer is passed as argument.

briefdescription.para: Initialize a device.

detaileddescription.para: A device whose initialization was deferred (by marking it as zephyr,deferred-init on devicetree) needs to be initialized manually via this call. De-initialized devices can also be initialized again via this call.

parameterdescription.para: device to be initialized.
Device is already initialized.
For other errors.

briefdescription.para: De-initialize a device.

detaileddescription.para: When a device is de-initialized, it will release any resources it has acquired (e.g. pins, memory, clocks, DMA channels, etc.) and its status will be left as in its reset state.
Note: this will be available if CONFIG_DEVICE_DEINIT_SUPPORT is enabled.

simplesect.para: It is the responsibility of the caller to ensure that the device is ready to be de-initialized.

parameterdescription.para: device to be de-initialized.
If successful
If device has not been initialized.
If device does not support de-initialization, or if the feature is not enabled (see CONFIG_DEVICE_DEINIT_SUPPORT)
For any other errors.

briefdescription.para: Flag value used to identify an unknown device.
Expands to the name of a global device object.

detaileddescription.para: Return the full name of a device object symbol created by DEVICE_DEFINE(), using the dev_id provided to DEVICE_DEFINE(). This is the name of the global variable storing the device structure, not a pointer to the string in the device::name field.
It is meant to be used for declaring extern symbols pointing to device objects before using the DEVICE_GET macro to get the device object.
This macro is normally only useful within device driver source code. In other situations, you are probably looking for device_get_binding().

parameterdescription.para: Device identifier.

simplesect.para: The full name of the device object defined by device definition macros.

memberdef.name: DEVICE_DEINIT_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: Z_DEVICE_STATE_DEFINE(dev_id); \ Z_DEVICE_DEFINE(DT_INVALID_NODE, dev_id, name, init_fn, deinit_fn, 0U, \ pm, data, config, level, prio, api, \ &Z_DEVICE_STATE_NAME(dev_id))

briefdescription.para: Create a device object and set it up for boot time initialization, with de-init capabilities.

detaileddescription.para: This macro defines a device that is automatically configured by the kernel during system initialization. This macro should only be used when the device is not being allocated from a devicetree node. If you are allocating a device from a devicetree node, use DEVICE_DT_DEINIT_DEFINE() or DEVICE_DT_INST_DEINIT_DEFINE() instead.
Note: deinit_fn will only be used if CONFIG_DEVICE_DEINIT_SUPPORT is enabled.

parameterdescription.para: A unique token which is used in the name of the global device structure as a C identifier.

A string name for the device, which will be stored in device::name. This name can be used to look up the device with device_get_binding(). This must be less than Z_DEVICE_MAX_NAME_LEN characters (including terminating NULL) in order to be looked up from user mode.
Pointer to the device's initialization function, which will be run by the kernel during system initialization. Can be NULL.
Pointer to the device's de-initialization function. Can be NULL. It must release any acquired resources (e.g. pins, bus, clock...) and leave the device in its reset state.
Pointer to the device's power management resources, a pm_device, which will be stored in device::pm field. Use NULL if the device does not use PM.
Pointer to the device's private mutable data, which will be stored in device::data.
Pointer to the device's private constant data, which will be stored in device::config.
The device's initialization level (PRE_KERNEL_1, PRE_KERNEL_2 or POST_KERNEL).
The device's priority within its initialization level. See SYS_INIT() for details.
Pointer to the device's API structure. Can be NULL.

memberdef.name: DEVICE_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: DEVICE_DEINIT_DEFINE(dev_id, name, init_fn, NULL, pm, data, config, \ level, prio, api)

briefdescription.para: Create a device object and set it up for boot time initialization.
Return a string name for a devicetree node.

detaileddescription.para: This macro returns a string literal usable as a device's name from a devicetree node identifier.

parameterdescription.para: The devicetree node identifier.

simplesect.para: The value of the node's label property, if it has one. Otherwise, the node's full name in node-name@unit-address form.

memberdef.name: DEVICE_DT_DEINIT_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: Z_DEVICE_STATE_DEFINE(Z_DEVICE_DT_DEV_ID(node_id)); \ Z_DEVICE_DEFINE(node_id, Z_DEVICE_DT_DEV_ID(node_id), \ DEVICE_DT_NAME(node_id), init_fn, deinit_fn, \ Z_DEVICE_DT_FLAGS(node_id), pm, data, config, level, \ prio, api, \ &Z_DEVICE_STATE_NAME(Z_DEVICE_DT_DEV_ID(node_id)), \ __VA_ARGS__)

briefdescription.para: Create a device object from a devicetree node identifier and set it up for boot time initialization.

detaileddescription.para: This macro defines a device that is automatically configured by the kernel during system initialization. The global device object's name as a C identifier is derived from the node's dependency ordinal or hash. device::name is set to DEVICE_DT_NAME(node_id).

The device is declared with extern visibility, so a pointer to a global device object can be obtained with DEVICE_DT_GET(node_id) from any source file that includes <zephyr/device.h> (even from extensions, when CONFIG_LLEXT_EXPORT_SYMBOL_GROUP_DEVICE is enabled). Before using the pointer, the referenced object should be checked using device_is_ready().
Note: deinit_fn will only be used if CONFIG_DEVICE_DEINIT_SUPPORT is enabled.

parameterdescription.para: The devicetree node identifier.
Pointer to the device's initialization function, which will be run by the kernel during system initialization. Can be NULL.
Pointer to the device's de-initialization function. Can be NULL. It must release any acquired resources (e.g. pins, bus, clock...) and leave the device in its reset state.
Pointer to the device's power management resources, a pm_device, which will be stored in device::pm. Use NULL if the device does not use PM.
Pointer to the device's private mutable data, which will be stored in device::data.
Pointer to the device's private constant data, which will be stored in device::config field.
The device's initialization level (PRE_KERNEL_1, PRE_KERNEL_2 or POST_KERNEL).
The device's priority within its initialization level. See SYS_INIT() for details.
Pointer to the device's API structure. Can be NULL.

memberdef.name: DEVICE_DT_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: DEVICE_DT_DEINIT_DEFINE(node_id, init_fn, NULL, pm, data, config, \ level, prio, api, __VA_ARGS__)

briefdescription.para: Create a device object from a devicetree node identifier and set it up for boot time initialization.
Like DEVICE_DT_DEINIT_DEFINE(), but uses an instance of a DT_DRV_COMPAT compatible instead of a node identifier.

parameterdescription.para: Instance number. The node_id argument to DEVICE_DT_DEFINE() is set to DT_DRV_INST(inst).
Other parameters as expected by DEVICE_DT_DEFINE().

briefdescription.para: Like DEVICE_DT_DEFINE(), but uses an instance of a DT_DRV_COMPAT compatible instead of a node identifier.

parameterdescription.para: Instance number. The node_id argument to DEVICE_DT_DEFINE() is set to DT_DRV_INST(inst).
Other parameters as expected by DEVICE_DT_DEFINE().

briefdescription.para: The name of the global device object for node_id.

detaileddescription.para: Returns the name of the global device structure as a C identifier. The device must be allocated using DEVICE_DT_DEFINE() or DEVICE_DT_INST_DEFINE() for this to work.
This macro is normally only useful within device driver source code. In other situations, you are probably looking for DEVICE_DT_GET().

parameterdescription.para: Devicetree node identifier

simplesect.para: The name of the device object as a C identifier

briefdescription.para: Get a device reference from a devicetree node identifier.

detaileddescription.para: Returns a pointer to a device object created from a devicetree node, if any device was allocated by a driver.

If no such device was allocated, this will fail at linker time. If you get an error that looks like undefined reference to __device_dts_ord_<N>, that is what happened. Check to make sure your device driver is being compiled, usually by enabling the Kconfig options it requires.

parameterdescription.para: A devicetree node identifier

simplesect.para: A pointer to the device object created for that node

briefdescription.para: Get a device reference for an instance of a DT_DRV_COMPAT compatible.

detaileddescription.para: This is equivalent to DEVICE_DT_GET(DT_DRV_INST(inst)).

parameterdescription.para: DT_DRV_COMPAT instance number

simplesect.para: A pointer to the device object created for that instance

briefdescription.para: Get a device reference from a devicetree compatible.

detaileddescription.para: If an enabled devicetree node has the given compatible and a device object was created from it, this returns a pointer to that device.
If there no such devices, this returns NULL.
If there are multiple, this returns an arbitrary one.
If this returns non-NULL, the device must be checked for readiness before use, e.g. with device_is_ready().

parameterdescription.para: lowercase-and-underscores devicetree compatible

simplesect.para: a pointer to a device, or NULL

briefdescription.para: Get a device reference from a devicetree compatible.

detaileddescription.para: If an enabled devicetree node has the given compatible and a device object was created from it, this returns a pointer to that device.
If there are no such devices, this will fail at compile time.
If there are multiple, this returns an arbitrary one.
If this returns non-NULL, the device must be checked for readiness before use, e.g. with device_is_ready().

parameterdescription.para: lowercase-and-underscores devicetree compatible

simplesect.para: a pointer to a device

briefdescription.para: Utility macro to obtain an optional reference to a device.

detaileddescription.para: If the node identifier refers to a node with status okay, this returns DEVICE_DT_GET(node_id). Otherwise, it returns NULL.

parameterdescription.para: devicetree node identifier

simplesect.para: a device reference for the node identifier, which may be NULL.

briefdescription.para: Get a device reference from a devicetree phandles by idx.

detaileddescription.para: Returns a pointer to a device object referenced by a phandles property, by idx.

parameterdescription.para: A devicetree node identifier
lowercase-and-underscores property with type phandle, phandles, or phandle-array
logical index into phs, which must be zero if phs has type phandle

simplesect.para: A pointer to the device object created for that node

briefdescription.para: Obtain a pointer to a device object by name.

detaileddescription.para: Return the address of a device object created by DEVICE_DEFINE(), using the dev_id provided to DEVICE_DEFINE().

parameterdescription.para: Device identifier.

simplesect.para: A pointer to the device object created by DEVICE_DEFINE()

briefdescription.para: Declare a static device object.

detaileddescription.para: This macro can be used at the top-level to declare a device, such that DEVICE_GET() may be used before the full declaration in DEVICE_DEFINE().

This is often useful when configuring interrupts statically in a device's init or per-instance config function, as the init function itself is required by DEVICE_DEFINE() and use of DEVICE_GET() inside it creates a circular dependency.

parameterdescription.para: Device identifier.

briefdescription.para: Get a init_entry reference from a devicetree node.

parameterdescription.para: A devicetree node identifier

simplesect.para: A pointer to the init_entry object created for that node

briefdescription.para: Get a init_entry reference from a device identifier.

parameterdescription.para: Device identifier.

simplesect.para: A pointer to the init_entry object created for that device

briefdescription.para: Writes a "device not ready" warning message to the log.

detaileddescription.para: Writes a "device not ready" warning message to the log using the device name as reference, meant to be used in device_is_ready checks.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to a struct device.

briefdescription.para: Writes a "device not ready" error message to the log.

detaileddescription.para: Writes a "device not ready" error message to the log using the device name as reference, meant to be used in device_is_ready checks.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to a struct device.

briefdescription.para: Device Model.
